DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CUSTOM SCAFFOLD'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES OF CROSS DEFENDANT BAY POINT CONTROL INC DBA MARINA MECHANICAL TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS SET ONE. MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED. NO TIMELY OPPOSITION FILED. RFP 1: THE PRIVACY OBJECTION IS OVERRULED. IN WILLIAMS V. SUP. CT. (2017) 3 CAL.5TH 531, 554-55, THE SUPREME COURT PERMITTED DISCOVERY OF SOME PERSONNEL INFORMATION OF NON-PARTIES BASED ON CERTAIN SHOWINGS. HERE, THE RFP SEEKS THE RECORDS OF PLAINTIFF, WHO IS A PARTY TO THIS LAWSUIT AND COULD EITHER REQUEST THE DOCUMENTS IN DISCOVERY HIMSELF OR SEEK A PROTECTIVE ORDER UNDER CCP 2031.060 IF HE OBJECTS TO THE RFP. THE PARTIES MAY MEET-AND-CONFER ABOUT A PROTECTIVE ORDER OR OTHER STIPULATION THAT WILL LIMIT USE OF THE PERSONNEL RECORDS TO THE HANDLING OF THIS CASE. RFP 3: THE RFP PERMISSIBLY REQUESTED PRODUCTION OF NATIVE FORMAT ELECTRONIC FILES. CCP 2031.030.(A)(2). MARINA MECHANICAL'S RESPONSE DID NOT OBJECT TO THE REQUESTED FORM OF PRODUCTION. CCP 2031.210(D). MARINA MECHANICAL IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE COMPLETE ELECTRONICALLY STORED INFORMATION, WITH METADATA INTACT, WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE HEARING. RFP 5: TO THE EXTENT THE RESPONSE TO THIS RFP SEEKS PERSONNEL RECORDS OR IS BASED ON THE PRIVACY OBJECTION, THE COURT ADOPTS HERE ITS RULING ON RFP 1. TO THE EXTENT THE THIS RFP RELATES TO ELECTRONIC DOCUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTS HERE ITS RULING ON RFP 3 REGARDING FORM OF PRODUCTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBORROWMAN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Cross Defendant Bay Point Control Inc Dba Marina Mechaical To Request For Production Of Documents Set One
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearance;

DEFENDANT CUSTOM SCAFFOLD's Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Cross Defendant Bay Point Control Inc Dba Marina Mechaical To Request For Production Of Documents Set One is Off calendar per request of the moving party (email dated 6/19/19). =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Cross Defendant Bay Point Control Inc Dba Marina Mechanical To Request For Production Of Documents Set One
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CUSTOM SCAFFOLD's Motion To Compel Further Responses Of Cross Defendant Bay Point Control Inc Dba Marina Mechanical To Request For Production Of Documents Set One.

Motion to compel GRANTED. No timely opposition filed.

RFP 1: The privacy objection is overruled. In Williams v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 554-55, the Supreme Court permitted discovery of some personnel information of non-parties based on certain showings. Here, the RFP seeks the records of Plaintiff, who is a party to this lawsuit and could either request the documents in discovery himself or seek a protective order under CCP 2031.060 if he objects to the RFP. The parties may meet-and-confer about a protective order or other stipulation that will limit use of the personnel records to the handling of this case.

RFP 3: The RFP permissibly requested production of native format electronic files. CCP 2031.030.(a)(2). Marina Mechanical's response did not object to the requested form of production. CCP 2031.210(d). Marina Mechanical is ordered to produce complete electronically stored information, with metadata intact, within 10 days of the hearing.

RFP 5: To the extent the response to this RFP seeks personnel records or is based on the privacy objection, the Court adopts here its ruling on RFP 1. To the extent the this RFP relates to electronic documents, the Court adopts here its ruling on RFP 3 regarding form of production.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
